skill.name: literature-experiment-extract
skill.description: Extract experimental models, experimental methods, and biomarker information from paper Markdown (typically produced by PDF-to-Markdown tools) when a user provides paper Markdown and needs a structured, evidence-backed summary (1 Markdown + 3 CSVs).
skill.summary: Extract experimental models, methods, and biomarkers from research papers with page-level evidence tracing.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill is documentation-only with no executable code. All 46 static findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting (backtick code blocks resembling shell commands), a legitimate GitHub repository URL, and text references in audit metadata. The skill provides structured guidance for extracting experimental data from research paper Markdown. All 4 files scanned (491 lines) are documentation or JSON audit data. Previous evaluation confirms all security gates passed.

medium_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Resemble Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Backtick-formatted code blocks in Markdown files (SKILL.md and references/guide.md) match the pattern for shell backtick execution. These are standard Markdown code blocks showing example output file paths (e.g., outputs/{Paper Abbreviation}-models.csv) and usage instructions. No actual shell commands exist anywhere in this documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Legitimate Source Repository Link
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The URL at SKILL.md:7 is a GitHub repository URL used for source attribution. It is never executed or fetched during skill operation. This is a standard practice for open-source documentation to indicate the skill origin.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance in Audit Metadata
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged JSON text content in literature-experiment-extract_audit_result_v1.json as system reconnaissance. This file is a structured audit report with evaluation metadata (scores, pass/fail gates, recommendations). No system reconnaissance or information gathering behavior exists.

content.user_title: Extract Experiments and Biomarkers from Research Papers
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually extracting experimental models, methods, and biomarkers from PDF papers. This skill automates structured extraction from paper Markdown into one summary and three CSV files with page-level evidence tracing.
actual_capabilities: Extract cell lines, animal models, strains, and genotypes from paper text
Identify experimental methods including assays, protocols, instruments, and conditions
Compile biomarkers, genes, proteins, and measurement readouts from study text
Produce one structured Markdown summary with evidence citations organized by page
Generate three CSV files (models, methods, biomarkers) for downstream analysis
Merge duplicate entities across pages while preserving multiple evidence excerpts
limitations: Requires paper text pre-converted to Markdown format (PDF-to-Markdown conversion is not included)
Cannot infer missing information; only extracts explicitly stated content
Output language is Chinese by default, which may not suit all users
Does not validate scientific accuracy of extracted information

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review Preparation
use_cases.description: A research assistant processing 20 immunology papers compiles all cell models and biomarkers into structured tables for meta-analysis. The skill extracts entities page by page, reducing manual review time from hours to minutes.

use_cases.title: Experimental Design Reference Gathering
use_cases.description: A principal investigator reviewing literature for a new study on cancer biomarkers collects methods and assay types across 15 recent publications. The skill produces standardized CSV outputs for easy comparison across studies.

use_cases.title: Laboratory Data Management
use_cases.description: A lab manager maintains a database of protocols and biomarkers used in published studies relevant to the lab's research focus. The skill extracts structured data from papers and produces evidence-backed records for the lab knowledge base.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Model Extraction from Single Paper
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a research paper in Markdown format about [topic]. Please extract all experimental models mentioned (cell lines, animal models, strains, genotypes) using the literature-experiment-extract skill. Follow the extraction rules in references/guide.md and use the template from assets/template.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user extracting models from one paper

prompt_templates.title: Full Extraction with Methods and Biomarkers
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the literature-experiment-extract skill, process this paper Markdown and extract three categories: (1) experimental models, (2) experimental methods and protocols, (3) biomarkers and detection indicators. Output the results as one Markdown summary and three CSV files following the specification in SKILL.md. Organize evidence by page number.
prompt_templates.scenario: User performing complete extraction on a single paper

prompt_templates.title: Batch Processing with Specific Output Language
prompt_templates.prompt: For each of the attached paper Markdown files, use the literature-experiment-extract skill to extract models, methods, and biomarkers. Output all field content in English (except proper nouns and abbreviations). Save each paper's results to outputs/ with the paper abbreviation as filename prefix. Maintain page-level evidence tracing.
prompt_templates.scenario: User processing multiple papers with English output preference

prompt_templates.title: Custom Extraction with Deduplication Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the literature-experiment-extract skill to this paper Markdown. Pay special attention to the deduplication rules in references/guide.md: merge identical entities that appear on different pages but retain all evidence excerpts. For entities with different conditions or groupings, split into separate records. Verify that evidence excerpts are complete original sentences.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user requiring strict deduplication handling

output_examples.input: Paper Markdown about a COVID-19 study describing Vero cell infection assays and IL-6 cytokine measurements.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a Markdown summary with three sections (Models, Methods, Biomarkers) listing Vero cells under Models with the evidence excerpt and page number, viral infection assays under Methods, and IL-6 levels under Biomarkers. Three CSV files are generated with matching structured data.

output_examples.input: Paper Markdown for a cancer immunotherapy study mentioning C57BL/6 mice, flow cytometry, and PD-1 expression levels.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies C57BL/6 mice as the animal model with strain and sex details, flow cytometry as a cellular method under Methods, and PD-1 as a biomarker with expression measurement context. All entries include a full sentence excerpt from the source text and the page number where the information was found.

best_practices: Provide paper Markdown with page headers (Page XX format) for best evidence tracing results across all output files
Verify extracted entities match the original paper content and that no false inferences were added for missing data
Review CSV output column alignment and UTF-8 encoding before using extracted data in downstream analysis tools
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for papers that have not been converted to Markdown; PDF-to-Markdown conversion must be done separately
Do not ask the skill to infer or supplement missing experimental details; it will only extract explicitly stated information
Do not modify the evidence excerpts in the CSV files; original language excerpts must stay intact for traceability

faq.question: What input format does this skill require?
faq.answer: The skill requires research paper text in Markdown format. Papers are typically converted from PDF using PDF-to-Markdown tools before using this skill.

faq.question: What output files does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces four files: one Markdown summary and three CSV files (models, methods, biomarkers). All files are saved in an outputs/ directory with the paper abbreviation as prefix.

faq.question: Does the skill verify scientific accuracy?
faq.answer: No. The skill only extracts information explicitly stated in the paper text. It does not validate whether the extracted data is scientifically accurate or complete.

faq.question: What language is the output in?
faq.answer: By default, the field content is in Chinese. Evidence excerpts remain in the original language of the source paper. You can request a different output language when using the skill.

faq.question: How are duplicate entities handled?
faq.answer: When the same entity appears on multiple pages, the skill merges them into one record and retains all evidence excerpts with their page numbers. Entities with different conditions are split into separate records.

faq.question: Does this skill execute any code or make network requests?
faq.answer: No. This skill is documentation-only. It provides structured instructions for an AI assistant to extract and organize information. It does not execute scripts or make external network requests.
